title: Fried Fish in Cedar Rapids
headline: Fried fish, made to order in Cedar Rapids
body: Captains Fish and Chicken fries catfish, perch, tilapia, cod, and shrimp to order, with cornmeal breading and fresh-ground lemon pepper. Counter-service on the northeast side, takeout-first.

sections.heading: Our fish
sections.text: Catfish is the most-ordered; we also fry perch, tilapia, cod, and shrimp — all breaded and fried fresh when you order.

faqs.question: What fried fish do you serve?
faqs.answer: Catfish, perch, tilapia, cod, and shrimp.

faqs.question: Where are you located?
faqs.answer: 1616 1st Ave NE, on the northeast side of Cedar Rapids.

seo.title: Fried Fish in Cedar Rapids — Captains Fish and Chicken
seo.description: Cornmeal-breaded catfish, perch, tilapia, cod, and shrimp fried to order on the northeast side of Cedar Rapids.

title: Fried Chicken in Cedar Rapids
headline: Fried chicken, wings, and tenders in Cedar Rapids
body: Captains fries chicken, wings (bone-in and boneless), tenders, gizzards, and liver to order. Counter-service and takeout-first at 1616 1st Ave NE.

sections.heading: Chicken done right
sections.text: Bone-in fried chicken, tenders, and both bone-in and boneless wings, breaded and fried fresh when you order.

faqs.question: Do you have both fish and chicken?
faqs.answer: Yes — Captains does both fish and chicken, made fresh to order.

faqs.question: Do you serve wings?
faqs.answer: Yes — bone-in and boneless.

seo.title: Fried Chicken in Cedar Rapids — Captains Fish and Chicken
seo.description: Fried chicken, wings, and tenders made to order on the northeast side of Cedar Rapids. Counter-service, takeout-first.

title: Soul Food in Cedar Rapids
headline: Southern soul food on the northeast side
body: Fried catfish, chicken, gizzards, liver, and Southern sides like hush puppies and okra — family-owned soul food, made fresh to order in Cedar Rapids.

sections.heading: Southern classics
sections.text: From catfish and gizzards to hush puppies and fried okra, Captains cooks Southern soul-food staples to order.

faqs.question: What makes it soul food?
faqs.answer: Southern-style fried fish and chicken with classic sides like hush puppies, okra, and cornbread-style breading.

seo.title: Soul Food in Cedar Rapids — Captains Fish and Chicken
seo.description: Family-owned Southern soul food — fried catfish, chicken, gizzards, and classic sides made to order in northeast Cedar Rapids.

title: Seafood in Cedar Rapids
headline: Fried seafood, made to order
body: Catfish, perch, tilapia, cod, shrimp, calamari, and crab cakes — fried seafood made to order at Captains on 1st Ave NE.

sections.heading: On the seafood menu
sections.text: Beyond catfish and shrimp, we fry perch, tilapia, cod, calamari, and crab cakes, plus combination seafood baskets.

faqs.question: What seafood do you have?
faqs.answer: Catfish, perch, tilapia, cod, shrimp, calamari, crab cakes, and combination baskets.

seo.title: Seafood in Cedar Rapids — Captains Fish and Chicken
seo.description: Fried catfish, shrimp, perch, cod, calamari, and crab cakes made to order in northeast Cedar Rapids.

title: Best Fried Catfish in Cedar Rapids
headline: Our catfish is the favorite
body: Fried catfish is Captains' most-ordered item — cornmeal breading with fresh-ground lemon pepper, fried fresh when you order. According to owner Ray Aqel, the catfish takes on a flavor profile close to the fried chicken.

sections.heading: How we do catfish
sections.text: Cornmeal breading, fresh-ground lemon-pepper seasoning, and frying to order — that's the catfish regulars come back for.

faqs.question: What's your most popular dish?
faqs.answer: The fried catfish is the most-ordered item on the menu.

faqs.question: How is the catfish seasoned?
faqs.answer: Cornmeal breading with fresh-ground lemon pepper, fried to order.

seo.title: Best Fried Catfish in Cedar Rapids — Captains Fish and Chicken
seo.description: Captains' most-ordered dish: cornmeal-breaded fried catfish with fresh-ground lemon pepper, fried to order in Cedar Rapids.

title: How We Cook
headline: Made fresh to order
body: At Captains, fish and chicken are breaded and fried when you order — not held under a lamp. Our fish gets a cornmeal breading with fresh-ground lemon pepper. Because it's cooked to order, calling ahead means it's hot when you arrive.

sections.heading: Fresh to order
sections.text: Frying to order takes a few extra minutes, which is why we recommend calling ahead at (319) 221-1040.

sections.heading: Cornmeal and lemon pepper
sections.text: The fish breading is cornmeal with fresh-ground lemon pepper — the seasoning owner Ray Aqel built the concept around.

faqs.question: Is the food made to order?
faqs.answer: Yes — everything is breaded and fried fresh when you order.

faqs.question: Why call ahead?
faqs.answer: Because it's cooked to order, calling ahead means your food is ready and hot when you arrive.

seo.title: How We Cook — Captains Fish and Chicken
seo.description: Fish and chicken breaded and fried to order, with cornmeal and fresh-ground lemon pepper. Call ahead so it's hot when you arrive.

title: First Time? What to Order
headline: New to Captains? Start here
body: First visit? Start with the fried catfish — it's the most-ordered item. Fried chicken and wings are close behind, cheese curds are a favorite side, and chocolate cake finishes the meal.

sections.heading: Crowd favorites
sections.text: Catfish, fried chicken, wings, cheese curds, and chocolate cake are the ones regulars come back for.

faqs.question: What should I order first?
faqs.answer: The fried catfish — it's the most popular dish. Add cheese curds and, if you have room, chocolate cake.

seo.title: What to Order at Captains Fish and Chicken
seo.description: New to Captains in Cedar Rapids? Start with the fried catfish, wings, cheese curds, and chocolate cake.

title: About Captains Fish and Chicken
headline: Family-owned, made fresh to order
body: Captains Fish and Chicken is a family-owned, counter-service soul-food restaurant at 1616 1st Ave NE in Cedar Rapids. Owner Ray Aqel has run the same fish-and-chicken concept in Minnesota, Illinois, and Michigan and has managed restaurants since 2006; Cedar Rapids is his first Iowa location. Everything is made fresh to order.

sections.heading: The owner
sections.text: Ray Aqel built the Captains concept across three states before bringing it to Cedar Rapids, cooking fish and chicken to order with a cornmeal-and-lemon-pepper breading.

faqs.question: Who owns Captains?
faqs.answer: Ray Aqel, who has run the same concept in Minnesota, Illinois, and Michigan and has managed restaurants since 2006.

faqs.question: When did the Cedar Rapids location open?
faqs.answer: October 2024 — it's the concept's first Iowa location.

seo.title: About — Captains Fish and Chicken, Cedar Rapids
seo.description: Family-owned soul-food restaurant on the northeast side of Cedar Rapids. Owner Ray Aqel has run the concept since 2006; Cedar Rapids is the first Iowa location.

title: Frequently Asked Questions
headline: Captains Fish and Chicken — FAQ
body: Answers to common questions about ordering, hours, and what's on the menu at Captains Fish and Chicken.

faqs.question: What are your hours?
faqs.answer: Mon–Thu 11am–10pm, Fri–Sat 11am–11pm, and Sun 11am–8pm.

faqs.question: Where are you located?
faqs.answer: 1616 1st Ave NE, on the northeast side of Cedar Rapids, with onsite parking.

faqs.question: What's the most popular dish?
faqs.answer: The fried catfish is the most-ordered item.

faqs.question: Is the food made to order?
faqs.answer: Yes — fish and chicken are breaded and fried fresh when you order. Call ahead at (319) 221-1040 for faster pickup.

faqs.question: Do you have seating, or is it takeout only?
faqs.answer: There is a dining room at the 1616 storefront, but Captains is counter-service and most orders go out — takeout-first. Call ahead for the fastest pickup.

faqs.question: Do you take reservations?
faqs.answer: No — Captains is a counter-service restaurant.

faqs.question: Can I get delivery?
faqs.answer: Yes — delivery is available through DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, and Postmates during open hours.

seo.title: FAQ — Captains Fish and Chicken, Cedar Rapids
seo.description: Hours, location, ordering, delivery, and menu questions for Captains Fish and Chicken in Cedar Rapids.

title: How to Order
headline: How to order from Captains
body: Captains is counter-service and takeout-first. There is a dining room at the 1616 storefront, but most orders go out. Because everything is fried to order, calling ahead at (319) 221-1040 is the fastest way to order. Delivery is available through DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, and Postmates.

sections.heading: Call ahead
sections.text: Call (319) 221-1040, place your order, and it's ready hot when you arrive at 1616 1st Ave NE.

sections.heading: Delivery
sections.text: Order delivery through DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, or Postmates during open hours.

faqs.question: What's the fastest way to order?
faqs.answer: Call ahead at (319) 221-1040 — since food is fried to order, calling ahead means it's ready when you arrive.

faqs.question: Do you deliver?
faqs.answer: Yes — through DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, and Postmates during open hours.

faqs.question: Can I eat there?
faqs.answer: There's a dining room at the storefront, but Captains is counter-service and takeout-first.

seo.title: How to Order — Captains Fish and Chicken
